title: Persist TDigest on shutdown and short runs
description: hashmeter.py updates the TDigest in memory on every sample but only writes data/hashmeter.json when td.n % 1000 == 0 at lines 104-109. Signal handling only sets running false at lines 50-52, so short runs or interrupted sessions can leave only timing_values_*.log and no hashmeter.json for analysis/visualization, despite README.md claiming clean shutdown preservation.
design: Keep the fix local to HashMeter: add one explicit persistence path used by periodic compression and shutdown/error handling. Avoid broad architecture changes for this script repo.
acceptance_criteria:
- hashmeter.py writes the current TDigest before exiting normally, on SIGINT/SIGTERM, and after runtime errors when measurements were collected
- Short runs produce a usable hashmeter.json without waiting for 1000 samples
- Existing timing_values_*.log behavior remains intact
- README/AGENTS guidance is updated if persistence behavior changes
notes: Relevant files: hashmeter.py, README.md/readme.md, AGENTS.md if docs change. Verification should include a temp data-dir run with interval 0 or 1 that is stopped quickly, then analyze_hashmeter.py -f against the generated hashmeter.json.
close_reason: Added shared TDigest persistence path, persisted on run cleanup after measurements, and covered short-run persistence with unittest.

title: Document and regenerate visualization image
description: Add documented usage for regenerating the README visualization image and run the visualizer to update the tracked PNG from current hashmeter data.
design: Keep the CLI simple and consistent with the existing argparse approach: add an optional output path rather than introducing a broader plotting/export framework.
acceptance_criteria:
- visualize_hashmeter.py can write hashtimer-analysis-chart.png without relying only on an interactive window
- README.md explains how to regenerate the tracked chart image
- hashtimer-analysis-chart.png is regenerated from current data
- Relevant tests and syntax checks pass
- Changes are committed and pushed to origin/main
notes: Relevant files: visualize_hashmeter.py, test_visualize_hashmeter.py, README.md, hashtimer-analysis-chart.png. Suggested verification: unittest for visualization CLI, py_compile, run visualizer with --file data/hashmeter.json --output hashtimer-analysis-chart.png.

title: Clean up setup dependency installation
description: setup.sh installs dependencies with an ad hoc uv pip command at line 15 that includes duplicate matplotlib and the stdlib module name asyncio. The repo has no requirements or lock file, so setup is non-reproducible and can install unnecessary or confusing packages.
design: Prefer the smallest reproducibility improvement appropriate for a script repo: either a corrected setup.sh dependency list or a lightweight requirements file consumed by setup.sh.
acceptance_criteria:
- setup.sh installs each required third-party dependency once
- asyncio is not installed as a PyPI dependency unless a concrete need is documented
- Dependency source of truth is documented or made reproducible for this small repo
- README.md/readme.md setup instructions remain accurate
notes: Relevant files: setup.sh, README.md/readme.md, AGENTS.md if setup commands change. Suggested verification: review setup.sh and run python -m py_compile hashmeter.py analyze_hashmeter.py visualize_hashmeter.py; avoid broad packaging unless explicitly chosen.
close_reason: Added requirements.txt as dependency source of truth, updated setup.sh to install from it, removed asyncio/duplicate matplotlib from installation, and updated docs.

title: Ignore generated Python and HashTimer artifacts
description: .gitignore currently only ignores Beads/Dolt files. It does not ignore .venv, __pycache__, *.pyc, data/, hashmeter.json, or timing_values_*.log even though setup.sh creates .venv and hashmeter.py writes generated data by default.
design: Update .gitignore only; do not move generated data locations in this task.
acceptance_criteria:
- .gitignore ignores Python cache files, local virtual environments, and HashTimer generated data/log artifacts
- Existing tracked source/docs/assets remain unaffected
- AGENTS.md data guidance remains accurate
notes: Relevant file: .gitignore. Suggested verification: nit status after generating or checking patterns; avoid hiding source assets such as committed .jpg/.png files.
close_reason: Updated .gitignore for Python env/cache files and root HashTimer generated data/log artifacts; subagent quality review approved anchored patterns.

title: Guard analysis metrics against zero denominators
description: analyze_hashmeter.py computes stability_ratio = iqr / median_time and outlier_ratio = (max_time - p99) / iqr at lines 59-60. Sparse, identical, or near-identical timing distributions can make median_time or iqr zero and crash instead of reporting an interpretable analysis result.
design: Keep metric computation explicit in analyze_hashmeter_data; do not add a large stats abstraction for this small script.
acceptance_criteria:
- analyze_hashmeter.py handles zero median and zero IQR without ZeroDivisionError
- Output clearly reports unavailable/undefined ratios when denominators are zero
- Non-degenerate data keeps the existing metrics and formatting behavior
- Add a focused regression check using a minimal TDigest fixture with identical timings
notes: Relevant file: analyze_hashmeter.py. Suggested verification: run analysis against a generated JSON fixture with one or repeated identical centroid values, plus python -m py_compile analyze_hashmeter.py.
close_reason: Added regression test for identical timings and guarded undefined analysis ratios with N/A output and None return values.

title: Document maintainer onboarding for Beads and DOX
description: Capture the Beads server-mode and DOX initialization steps used in this repository in maintainer.md so future maintainers can reproduce or audit the onboarding process.
design: Keep this as a maintainer runbook: concise, chronological, and specific to the onboarding steps already performed in this repo.
acceptance_criteria:
- maintainer.md documents Beads server-mode initialization steps used here
- maintainer.md documents the DOX/AGENTS.md initialization flow used here
- The document records repo-specific commands and verification checks, not a generic tutorial
- Existing AGENTS.md guidance remains intact
notes: Expected file: maintainer.md. Suggested verification: read maintainer.md after writing, nit diff -- maintainer.md, bd ready --json.
close_reason: Captured Beads server-mode and DOX onboarding steps in maintainer.md and linked it from AGENTS.md.

title: Review repo for bugs and code smells
description: Analyze this small Python HashTimer repository for likely bugs, maintainability issues, best-practice gaps, documentation drift, setup problems, and repo hygiene issues. Produce actionable findings rather than broad rewrites.
design: Use a code-review mindset: prioritize bugs, data-loss risks, runtime failures, misleading docs, setup pitfalls, and small maintainability fixes. Keep recommendations proportional to a simple script repo with no packaging or test suite.
acceptance_criteria:
- Review hashmeter.py, analyze_hashmeter.py, visualize_hashmeter.py, setup.sh, README.md/readme.md, and AGENTS.md
- Identify concrete bugs or behavioral risks with file/line references where possible
- Identify practical code smells or best-practice gaps that matter for this repo size
- Separate verified findings from speculative ideas
- Create follow-up Beads issues for any actionable fixes that should be tracked separately
notes: Suggested verification commands during analysis: python -m py_compile hashmeter.py analyze_hashmeter.py visualize_hashmeter.py; inspect scripts manually; optionally run --help commands in an activated environment if dependencies are available. Out of scope: implementing fixes unless separate issues are claimed.
close_reason: Completed repository review, recorded findings in a comment, and created linked follow-up issues for actionable fixes.

comments.author: kha1n3vol3
comments.text: Review findings: python -m py_compile passed for all three scripts. Runtime CLI probes failed in this environment because .venv/dependencies are not installed (uv is available, but .venv is absent). Verified actionable issues filed: ht-s8i for TDigest persistence/short-run data loss, ht-r62 for analysis zero-denominator guards, ht-7df for setup dependency hygiene, ht-9md for generated artifact ignores, and ht-0jn for configurable visualization input. Additional lower-priority observations not separately filed: hashmeter.py has a duplicate td.compress() call at lines 105 and 107, unused Optional import at line 13, and Path.mkdir(exist_ok=True) at line 27 does not support nested --data-dir paths without parents=True.

title: Initialize DOX tree
description: Initialize this repository's DOX tree in AGENTS.md by replacing the uninitialized dox.md concept with a verified, compact index of actual repo-owned instruction scopes. Keep the result operational and avoid creating child AGENTS.md files unless the current repository structure justifies them.
design: Follow existing AGENTS.md Instruction Docs guidance: update the nearest owning AGENTS.md for durable instruction structure changes; keep instruction docs operational and omit broad framework text unless it changes how agents work here.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repository structure is scanned before editing
- AGENTS.md records the current DOX tree/index for this project
- No child AGENTS.md files are created unless there is a verified durable subtree with distinct local rules
- Existing Python repo guidance and Beads integration remain intact
- dox.md broad framework text is not copied wholesale
notes: Expected file: AGENTS.md. Reference: dox.md. Suggested verification: read AGENTS.md, glob **/AGENTS.md, nit diff -- AGENTS.md, bd ready --json.
close_reason: Initialized the DOX tree in AGENTS.md with root scope and an empty child index after scanning the repository.

title: Update Beads prefix and actor identity
description: Change this repository's Beads configuration so new issue IDs use the short prefix ht instead of hashtimer, and ensure Beads operations use the intended GitHub identity for the actor string.
design: Use Beads repository configuration rather than manually editing generated issue data. Keep the change scoped to Beads metadata/configuration; do not rewrite existing issue IDs unless Beads explicitly requires it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Beads issue prefix is changed from hashtimer to ht for future issues
- Beads actor identity is configured to match the repository owner's GitHub identity
- Existing issues remain readable after the prefix/configuration change
- The change is verified with a Beads command that shows the active configuration or by creating/inspecting a test-safe issue if no config display command exists
notes: Expected files are under .beads/ only, plus any repo-local Beads/agent instruction file only if Beads tooling updates it automatically. Check bd help/config commands first. Suggested verification: bd doctor, bd ready --json, and a command showing the active prefix/actor if available.
close_reason: Updated Beads prefix to ht and default actor to GitHub login kha1n3vol3; verified config and renamed issues.

title: Review dox.md for AGENTS.md guidance
description: Review the untracked dox.md file and update AGENTS.md only with verified, high-signal repo-specific instructions that future OpenCode sessions would likely miss. Avoid copying generic advice or unverified claims.
design: Follow the AGENTS.md writing rules from the user request: every retained line should answer 'Would an agent likely miss this without help?' Trust executable repo sources over prose if dox.md conflicts with scripts or README files.
acceptance_criteria:
- dox.md is reviewed for repo-specific guidance worth preserving in AGENTS.md
- AGENTS.md is updated only if dox.md contains verified high-signal instructions not already covered
- Generic, speculative, or stale dox.md content is omitted
- Existing AGENTS.md Beads integration and Python repo guidance remain intact
notes: Expected files: AGENTS.md; reference input: dox.md. Out of scope: committing generated data, changing Python behavior, or adding broad process guidance. Suggested verification: read AGENTS.md after edits and run nit status to ensure only intended files changed.
close_reason: Reviewed dox.md and distilled only high-signal instruction-doc guidance into AGENTS.md.

title: Make visualization input configurable
description: visualize_hashmeter.py is hard-coded to read ./data/hashmeter.json at line 104, while analyze_hashmeter.py already accepts -f/--file. This prevents visualizing alternate data directories such as the /tmp paths recommended in AGENTS.md and makes README usage less flexible.
design: Mirror analyze_hashmeter.py's simple argparse approach; avoid adding a broader CLI framework.
acceptance_criteria:
- visualize_hashmeter.py accepts a file path argument, preferably -f/--file matching analyze_hashmeter.py
- Default behavior still reads data/hashmeter.json
- README.md visualization instructions document the option
- Existing log-scale default remains unchanged unless deliberately documented
notes: Relevant files: visualize_hashmeter.py, README.md/readme.md if duplicated docs are kept in sync. Suggested verification: python visualize_hashmeter.py --help and py_compile.
close_reason: Added -f/--file support to visualize_hashmeter.py, preserved default/log-scale behavior, documented usage, and covered CLI behavior with unittest.
